assertions.assertion: Code fetches the discovery document to resolve the introspection endpoint rather than hardcoding it
Code calls IntrospectTokenAsync (or POSTs to the introspection endpoint) with the API's ClientId and ClientSecret for authentication
assertions.rationale: SKILL.md Concept 5 shows IntrospectTokenAsync with ClientId and ClientSecret

assertions.assertion: Code checks the IsActive property (or 'active' field) of the introspection response to determine if the token is valid
Response explains that reference tokens are opaque identifiers (not self-contained JWTs) and require server-side validation via introspection
assertions.rationale: SKILL.md Concept 4 Reference Tokens section states 'Reference tokens are not self-contained JWTs. Instead, the access token is an opaque identifier. The API must call the introspection endpoint'

assertions.assertion: Response mentions that reference tokens enable immediate revocation unlike JWTs which are valid until expiry
assertions.rationale: SKILL.md Concept 4 lists 'You need immediate token revocation (JWT lifetimes are not revocable until expiry)' as a use case for reference tokens
